title: Cite a Source
summary: Give proper credit to a source you used in a paper or project.
learnerPromise: You will be able to list the key information about a source and format it correctly using a common citation style.
whyItMatters: Citing sources shows respect for other people's work and helps readers find the same information. Most schools require proper citations and may lower grades for missing or incorrect ones.
realLifeUses: Listing a book you used for a research paper in a bibliography
Adding an in-text citation when you quote a website in an essay
Creating a works cited page for a science project
Giving credit to an image or video you used in a presentation
materialsNeeded: A source to cite (book, article, or website)
Your teacher's preferred citation style (MLA, APA, or Chicago)
Citation guide or template (from your teacher, school, or library)
result: One correctly formatted citation for a source you used
youWillLearn: How to find the author, title, date, and publisher of a source
How to follow a citation style format your teacher expects
How to create an in-text citation and a full works cited entry
How to keep track of sources while researching so you do not lose them
miniChallenge: Pick one source you have used for school. Write a complete citation for it in the style your teacher requires (MLA, APA, or another format).
steps: Identify the type of source: book, website, article, or video.
Find the key facts: author, title, date published, and where it was published.
Look up the correct format for that source type in your citation guide.
Fill in the information in the exact order the format requires.
Double-check spelling, dates, and punctuation against the original source.
completionCriteria: Citation includes all required parts for the source type
Citation follows the correct order and punctuation for the style
Information matches the original source exactly
commonProblems: Missing information like the publication date or page number
Mixing up citation styles, such as using APA for an MLA assignment
Making up details when information is hard to find
tips: Write down source information as soon as you find it, before you forget
Many library websites and databases can generate a citation for you to check
If a website has no author, use the organization name or the article title
